supersedes: ONLY the every-query blind-human key and the autonomy-earning provisions of rejudge/phase2_canary_checker_governance_2026-07-23.json, for this canary epoch; every other provision (logging, fail-closed, dual-key dispatch mechanics, sealed metrics) carries forward

owner_instruction.record: rejudge/phase2_canary_owner_decision_2026-07-23.json (verbatim quotes; scope A; USD 40 cap approved)
owner_instruction.constraint: no human labeling at canary query volume; at most ~60 minutes of post-hoc owner audit

oracle_ruling.conditional_assent: execution of the frozen canary with a metadata-blinded Claude second gate is the best available containment design under the fixed owner constraint
oracle_ruling.dissent: the Claude gate is not an independent blind-human key; checker agreement does not validate either gate, does not satisfy the 149-cluster autonomy requirement, and does not authorize the main run

claude_gate.identity: claude-fable-5 acting as the metadata-blinded second policy gate; declared conflict: the reviewer authored the codebook, influenced checker development, knows the three worlds, and has read Stage-1 results
blindness_controls: reviewer context per query batch is FRESH and ISOLATED: no repository access, tools, retrieval, conversation memory, or cross-query discussion; input is exactly the frozen reviewer prompt (contract + unified codebook) and the immutable query payload (raw query + candidate_a + candidate_b)

excluded from the reviewer's view: checker verdicts/scores/rationales, arm identity, clean vs placebo mode, judge identity, debate outcome, oracle answers, dispatch decisions, stage results, DEV-350 labels/rationales, prior adjudication notes
clean and placebo queries share one metadata-free queue, shuffled within each causally available stage; no queue boundaries or meaning-bearing IDs exposed

output: ALLOW / REJECT / CONTRACT_AMBIGUOUS + controlling clause + one-sentence rationale; parse failure, timeout, missing clause, or ambiguity is non-ALLOW; decisions committed immutably (hash-chained) BEFORE the checker/Claude join and before any oracle dispatch
an exact retry inherits the committed decision by payload hash; a changed payload gets a new blinded decision; identical queries across arms share one committed decision
the reviewer adjudicates contract compliance only, never truth, usefulness, or anticipated debate effect; any metadata leak is a logged protocol deviation and the affected query fails closed
claude_gate.dispatch: oracle (or placebo responder) fires only on checker-ALLOW AND claude-ALLOW; every other combination produces the identical blocked-query transition in clean and placebo alike

estimand_redefinition: canary results estimate the debate system WITH THIS FROZEN CLAUDE GATE, not ungated oracle access and not the previously ratified human-gated system; intention-to-treat over all randomized cells; gate pass/intercept/ambiguity/clause distributions reported by arm, stage, question, and judge after unblinding; realized oracle exposure reported as a mediator, never as an exclusion basis; pre-registered sensitivity analyses for arm imbalance in gate rates and for queries overlapping DEV-350 or Stage-1 material; equal allow totals are not proof of non-differential selection
consistency_only: checker-vs-Claude agreement is consistency evidence about correlated automated gates; it is never reported as false-allow or recall measurement against ground truth
autonomy_quota: the 149-cluster quota text is preserved historically with status SUSPENDED/NOT_EVALUATED; no Claude-reviewed cluster increments it; the eligible increment this epoch is zero; the quota is neither failed nor satisfied
reserve: the 7-cluster holdout reserve and all prior artifacts remain sealed, dormant, and unchanged; their autonomy-validation use is marked NOT_EVALUATED_THIS_EPOCH

owner_audit.design: pre-seeded stratified sentinel audit generated BEFORE the owner sees any canary result; unit = unique immutable query-decision cluster; strata per consult #27 (real-oracle double-ALLOW dispatches; placebo double-ALLOW; checker-ALLOW x claude-REJECT; checker-ALLOW x claude-AMBIGUOUS; checker-REJECT x claude-ALLOW; checker-REJECT x claude-REJECT; checker-REJECT x claude-AMBIGUOUS); 40 seeded SRS from real dispatches, >=2 from every nonempty other stratum, remainder proportional; owner sees no strata, labels, modes, outcomes, or meaning-bearing IDs
owner_audit.estimator: stratum-weighted disagreement with conservative stratified 95% intervals (per-stratum exact hypergeometric with Bonferroni, weighted endpoints); exact finite-population one-sided upper bound on dispatched-query violations; owner AMBIGUOUS maps to non-ALLOW operationally while the three-way matrix is retained; NEVER a pooled binomial CI
owner_audit.honesty: 40 clean dispatched audits with zero violations support roughly a 7% one-sided upper bound, not a 5% claim; the writeup uses consult #27's template verbatim
owner_audit.declined_consequence: the raw run remains preserved and usable as artifacts, but no independently-audited-safety claim and no main-run unlock

main_run: nothing in this amendment or the canary's completion authorizes the ~19,680-query main run; its gating design is a separate future decision with its own consult and owner approval
note: Append-only; prospective; canary-specific. Reviewer model/build change mid-canary is a protocol deviation.
